system.rateLimitedUser: ⏳ You're sending commands too fast! Please wait **{retryAfter}s** before using another command.
system.rateLimitedGuild: ⏳ This community is sending too many commands! Please wait **{retryAfter}s** before using another command.

reminder.failedDelete: [REMINDER] Failed to delete reminder from store:

remindme.noInput:
❌ Please provide a time and reminder message.
Usage: `!remindme in 5 minutes Take a break`
Examples:
`!remindme in 30 minutes Check the oven`
`!remindme tomorrow at 9am Team meeting`
`!remindme in 2 hours Walk the dog`

❌ Could not parse time. Try something like:
`in 5 minutes`, `tomorrow at 3pm`, `15 seconds`
remindme.pastTime: ❌ Time must be in the future.

translate.noInput: Please provide a language code and text. Example: `!translate es Hello` or `!translate fr:es Bonjour`

roll.error: ❌ Invalid dice format. Try e.g. `2d20+6`, `d8`, or just `20`.

purge.discordOnly: ❌ The purge command is only available as a Discord slash command.

purge.tooMany: ❌ You can only purge up to **100** messages at a time.

ratelimit.notOwner: ❌ Only bot operators can change platform-wide rate limits.

ratelimit.unknownAction: ❌ Unknown action `{action}`. Use `system ratelimit` to view, or `system ratelimit user <n>`, `system ratelimit guild <n>`, or `system ratelimit reset`.

ratelimit.updatedGuild: ✅ Platform-wide guild limit set to **{limit}** commands per minute.

Example: `system presence dnd`

Example: `system status 🔧 Maintenance mode`

rep.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.
rep.usage: ❌ Usage: `!rep @user` to give a reputation point.
rep.selfRep: ❌ You can't give yourself a reputation point.
rep.givenDescription: **{giver}** gave a rep point to **{receiver}**! They now have **{rep} rep**.
rep.botRep: 🤖 You can't give reputation points to bots.
rep.disabled: ❌ The reputation system has been disabled in this server.
rep.dailyAllowanceExhausted: ⏳ You've used all **3** of your daily reputation tokens! Come back in 24 hours.
rep.targetLockout: 🔒 You've already repped this user recently. You can give them rep again in **{time}**.

profile.title: 👤 {username}

profile.noRep: This user hasn't earned any rep yet.

leaderboard.noRepData: ⭐ No rep data yet — use `!rep @user` to give rep!

xp.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.
xp.helpDescription:
**Subcommands:**
`xp set <user> <amount>` — Set a user's XP (admin)
`xp add <user> <amount>` — Add XP to a user (admin)
`xp globalnotify [on/off]` — Toggle server announcements (admin)
`xp bonus add <word> <xp>` — Add keyword bonus (admin)
`xp bonus remove <word>` — Remove keyword bonus (admin)
`xp bonus list` — List all keyword bonuses
xp.unknownSubcommand: ❌ Unknown subcommand `{sub}`. Use `!xp` to see available commands.
xp.noPermission: ❌ You don't have permission to use this subcommand.
xp.invalidAmount: ❌ Invalid XP amount. Please provide a positive number.
xp.addResult: ✅ Added **{amount} XP** to <@{userId}> — now at **{xp} XP** (Level {level})
xp.bonusNoBonuses: 📝 No keyword bonuses configured for this server.

levelnotify.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.

neofetch.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a community.

ratelimit.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.
ratelimit.noPermission: ❌ You need the **Manage Messages** permission to change rate limits.
ratelimit.unknownAction: ❌ Unknown action `{action}`. Use `!ratelimit` to see current limits, or `!ratelimit user <n>`, `!ratelimit guild <n>`, or `!ratelimit reset`.
ratelimit.invalidValue: ❌ Please provide a valid positive number.
ratelimit.updatedUser: ✅ Per-user rate limit set to **{limit}** commands per minute.
ratelimit.updatedGuild: ✅ Per-guild rate limit set to **{limit}** commands per minute.
ratelimit.updatedReset: ✅ Rate limits reset to environment defaults.
ratelimit.tooHighUser: ❌ Per-user limit can't exceed **{limit}** (the platform-wide maximum).
ratelimit.tooHighGuild: ❌ Per-guild limit can't exceed **{limit}** (the platform-wide maximum).
ratelimit.footer: Use !ratelimit user <n> or !ratelimit guild <n> to change

settings.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.
settings.noPermission: ❌ You need the **Manage Server** permission to change settings.

prefix.helpDescription: View or change the command prefix.
prefix.noPermission: ❌ You need the **Manage Server** permission to change the prefix.
prefix.unknownAction: ❌ Unknown action `{action}`. Use `!settings prefix` to view the current prefix, or `!settings prefix set <prefix>` to change it.
prefix.currentCustom:
⚙️ The current command prefix is `{prefix}`. Use `!settings prefix set <new-prefix>` to change it or `!settings prefix reset` to revert to `{defaultPrefix}`.

If another bot conflicts with that prefix, mention me instead:
{botMention} settings prefix set <prefix>
prefix.currentDefault:
⚙️ The default prefix `{prefix}` is currently being used. Use `!settings prefix set <prefix>` to set a custom one.

If another bot conflicts with that prefix, mention me instead:
{botMention} settings prefix set <prefix>
prefix.setUsage:
❌ Usage: `!settings prefix set <prefix>`
Example: `!settings prefix set ?`

If another bot conflicts, mention me instead:
{botMention} settings prefix set ?
prefix.tooLong: ❌ Prefix must be 5 characters or fewer.
prefix.setSuccess:
✅ Command prefix changed to `{prefix}`. Use `{prefix}` instead of `{defaultPrefix}` from now on.

Forgot your prefix? Mention me:
{botMention} settings prefix — check current prefix
{botMention} settings prefix reset — restore prefix to the default `{defaultPrefix}`
prefix.resetSuccess: ✅ Prefix reset to `{defaultPrefix}`. All commands now use the default prefix.

commands.helpDescription: List or toggle individual command availability.
commands.usage:
❌ Usage: `!settings commands list` — show all commands
`!settings commands disable <name>` — disable a command
`!settings commands enable <name>` — re-enable a command
commands.title: 📋 Command Availability
commands.footer: Use `!settings commands disable <name>` or `!settings commands enable <name>` to toggle
commands.disabled: ❌ `{command}` has been disabled. Use `!settings commands enable {command}` to re-enable it.

features.helpDescription: Enable or disable data-storing features (XP, rep).
features.usage:
❌ Usage: `!settings features` — list features
`!settings features enable xp` — enable XP
`!settings features disable xp` — disable XP
`!settings features enable rep` — enable rep
`!settings features disable rep` — disable rep
features.title: ⚙️ Feature Toggles
features.xpEnabled: ✅ XP and leveling have been enabled.
features.xpDisabled: ❌ XP and leveling have been disabled. Existing data is preserved. Use `!settings features xp enable` to re-enable.
features.repDisabled: ❌ Reputation has been disabled. Existing data is preserved. Use `!settings features rep enable` to re-enable.

clear.helpDescription: Clear stored data for this server (xp, rep, roles, or everything).
clear.usage:
❌ Usage: `!settings clear xp` — clear XP data
`!settings clear rep` — clear reputation data
`!settings clear roles` — clear reaction, join, and level role bindings
`!settings clear all` — clear everything
clear.xpDone: 🗑️ All XP and keyword bonus data for this server has been cleared.
clear.repDone: 🗑️ All reputation data for this server has been cleared.
clear.rolesDone: 🗑️ All reaction role, join role, and level role bindings for this server have been cleared.
clear.everythingDone: 🗑️ All bot data for this server has been cleared. This includes XP, reputation, and all role bindings.

tasks.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.
tasks.unknownSubcommand: ❌ Unknown subcommand `{sub}`. Available: list, show, create, rename, reschedule, retool, edit, pause, resume, delete, run, history.

help.title: 📋 Scheduled Tasks
help.descSchedule: Schedule automated actions for this server.
help.create: `!task create <name> [action] [when]` — Create a task or manual trigger
help.whenCron: Or raw cron like `0 9 * * 1` for power users.
help.tipScheduled: • `!task create greeting announce daily` creates an active scheduled task.
help.tipManual: • `!task create greeting announce` creates a manual-only trigger (no schedule).
help.tipDraft: • `!task create greeting` makes a draft — fill it, then run manually or add a schedule.
help.tipEdit: • `!task edit greeting message:Hello everyone` configures the action.

list.empty: 📋 No tasks configured. Use `!task create` to add one.
list.title: 📋 Tasks

create.draftDesc:
Draft **{name}** saved. It needs a few things before it can run.

Fill them in:
`!task reschedule {name} <when>` — Set a schedule (or skip for manual trigger)
`!task retool {name} <action>` — Choose what to do
`!task edit {name} set channel:#channel` — Set the target channel

Then: `!task run {name}`
create.tooFewArgs:
❌ Too few arguments.
• Scheduled: `!task create <name> <action> <when> [key:value...]`
• Manual: `!task create <name> <action> [key:value...]`
• Draft: `!task create <name>` and fill the rest with `edit`.
Examples:
`!task create greeting announce daily channel:#general message:Hello!`
`!task create greeting announce channel:#general message:Hello!`
`!task create greeting`
create.draftResult:
📝 Created **{name}** as a draft — still missing: {fields}.
Use `!task edit {name} key:value` to fill them in, {suffix} to activate.
create.title: ✅ Task Created

reschedule.usage: ❌ Usage: `!task reschedule <name> <when>` (e.g. `!task reschedule greeting daily at 2pm`)
reschedule.cleared: 🖐️ **{name}** is now a manual trigger — no schedule.

retool.success: ✅ **{name}** retooled to `{action}`. Old action config has been cleared — use `!task edit {name} <key>:<value>` to configure the new action.

edit.usage:
❌ Usage: `!task edit <name> <key>:<value>` (e.g. `!task edit greeting message:Hello everyone`)
Common keys: `channel`, `message`, `count`, `role`, `inactive_days`
To clear a value: `!task edit <name> <key>:` with nothing after the colon.
edit.formatError: ❌ Use `key:value` format (e.g. `!task edit greeting message:Hello everyone`)
edit.allFieldsManual: All fields set! Use `!task run {name}` to trigger it.
edit.allFieldsScheduled: All fields set! Use `!task resume {name}` to activate.

role.guildOnly: ❌ This command can only be used in a server.
role.noPermission: ❌ You need the **Manage Roles** permission to manage reaction roles.

add.description: Bind an emoji to a role on a message.
add.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role reaction add <message-id> <emoji> <role>`
Example: `!role reaction add 123456789 🎉 @Member`
add.invalidRole: ❌ Invalid role. Please mention a role (e.g. @Role) or provide a role ID.
add.invalidEmoji: ❌ That emoji isn't available to me. Please use a standard Unicode emoji or a custom emoji that I can access.

remove.description: Remove an emoji-to-role binding from a message.
remove.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role reaction remove <message-id> <emoji>`
Example: `!role reaction remove 123456789 🎉`
remove.notFound: ❌ No binding found for {emoji} on that message.

clear.description: Remove all bindings from a message.
clear.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role reaction clear <message-id>`
Example: `!role reaction clear 123456789`
clear.notFound: ❌ No bindings found on that message.

list.description: Show bindings, optionally filtered by message or page.
list.title: 📋 Reaction Role Bindings
list.empty: 📋 No reaction role bindings configured for this server.
list.footer: Page {page}/{totalPages} ({total} total) — use `!role reaction list [message_id] and/or [page]` to filter

add.description: Add a join-role binding with optional age conditions.
add.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role join add <role> [member_age_minutes] [account_age_minutes]`
Example: `!role join add @Member 60` — assigns @Member after 1 hour in the server.
Example: `!role join add @Member 60 1440` — after 1 hour + account must be 1 day old.
Example: `!role join add @Member` — assigns @Member immediately on join.
add.invalidRole: ❌ Invalid role. Please mention a role (e.g. @Role) or provide a role ID.
add.invalidAge: ❌ Invalid age value. Please provide a positive number of minutes.
add.alreadyBound: ❌ That role is already configured as a join role in this community.
add.maxBindings: ❌ This community already has {max} join roles, the maximum allowed.
add.maxMemberAge: Member age cannot exceed {max} minutes (7 days).
add.maxAccountAge: Account age cannot exceed {max} minutes (30 days).
add.success:
✅ <@&{roleId}> will now be assigned when members join.
Account age condition: **{accountAge}**
Member age condition: **{memberAge}**

remove.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role join remove <role>`
Example: `!role join remove @Member`
remove.invalidRole: ❌ Invalid role. Please mention a role (e.g. @Role) or provide a role ID.

list.title: 📋 Join Role Bindings
list.empty:
📋 No join roles configured for this server.
Use `!role join add <role>` to add one.

pending.title: ⏳ Pending Role Assignments
pending.footer: Showing {shown}/{count} · pending assignments are polled every 30s

add.description: Bind a role to a level. Users who reach this level get the role.
add.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role level add <level> <role>`
Example: `!role level add 5 @Member`.
add.invalidRole: ❌ Invalid role. Please mention a role (e.g. @Role) or provide a role ID.
add.invalidLevel: ❌ Invalid level. Please provide a positive number.
add.maxBindings: ❌ This community already has {max} level roles, the maximum allowed.
add.success: ✅ Level **{level}** → <@&{roleId}> set! Users who reach level {level} will get this role.

remove.usage:
❌ Usage: `!role level remove <role>`
Example: `!role level remove @Member`
remove.invalidRole: ❌ Invalid role. Please mention a role (e.g. @Role) or provide a role ID.

list.title: 📋 Level Role Bindings
list.empty:
📋 No level roles configured for this server.
Use `!role level add <level> <role>` to add one.
